mechanism_narrative: A rising CPIAUCSL together with a still-low FEDFUNDS rate implies real policy rates are deeply negative, which supports risk assets through easier financial conditions and a lower discount-rate hurdle for equities over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are negative in more than 55% of months when CPIAUCSL is rising year-over-year and FEDFUNDS is below CPI inflation, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises sharply above its recent trend, earnings expectations and cyclical demand weaken, so S&P 500 forward returns over the next month turn negative as recession risk is repriced.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign of the 20-day return conditional on the trigger is not negative in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A broad upswing in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates improving payroll income and retail demand, which lifts near-term revenue revisions and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive-signal state for PAYEMS and RSAFS fails to outperform the unconditional 5-day SPX return by at least 25 bps on average, or if the conditional hit rate falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in INDPRO below its recent level signals weakening industrial output and margin pressure, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month as cyclical earnings estimates are cut.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if SPX 20-day returns are not negative in at least 55% of trigger episodes, or if the average conditional return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 20 bps.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in HOUST after prior strength signals housing demand rollover, which transmits to weaker consumption and risk appetite and lowers S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of periods during falling HOUST trends, or if the conditional average return is not statistically below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS rate above a restrictive threshold signals tighter discounting and liquidity conditions, so S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days should be negative until policy turns easier.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not negative on average during FEDFUNDS > 4.5% episodes, or if the sign of the signal flips positive in more than one rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint pattern of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE identifies a labor-market deterioration that is stronger than either series alone, and this interaction should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-down/UNRATE-up state does not produce lower 20-day SPX returns than either marginal signal alone, or if its conditional hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising GDPC1 and RSAFS together indicate real activity and consumer spending are reaccelerating, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as analysts raise earnings forecasts.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint rise in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to deliver positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the conditional mean return is not above the unconditional mean by at least 30 bps.
